clinical_history: An elderly woman is noted to have a relatively fast heart rate. What is the rhythm?
diagnosis: The ECG shows atrial fibrillation with a rapid ventricular response. The mean (resting) ventricular rate over 10 seconds is about 114/minute and is irregular (range about 80-160/min), without any discernable pattern. Note the continuous atrial activity (lead V1).  The coarser fibrillatory waves may simulate P waves or atrial flutter waves, leading to mis-diagnosis. Non-specific ST-T changes are present as well here.
